rulings.Case Title: WALID MANDO VS. JEFFER, MANGELS, BUTLER & MARMARO LLP, A et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 15, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANTS EUGENE CHIARELLI AND ARTER & HADDEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: The motion for summary judgment, based on the statute of limitations, is denied because there are triable issues of fact as to when plaintiff actually sustained an injury. The sanction order and letters complaining about fees are too ambiguous to establish that plaintiff, at that time, suffered an injury. Jordache Enterprises v. Brobeck, Phelger & Harrison (1998) 18 Cal.4th 739, 743. Plaintiff's dismissal of the claims against Chiarelli does not bar the claims against defendant. Plaintiff's claim relates only to the period of time when he was represented by defendant. As to the bankruptcy assignment, the Bankruptcy Court has exclusive jurisdiction of this issue and defendant's argument is a collateral attack on the Bankruptcy Court's order. This court lacks jurisdiction to negate that order. Plaintiff objections 1 and 2 are overruled; 3 is sustained. Defendant's objection to paragraph 5 of the Balin Declaration is sustained and the objection to paragraph 7 is sustained. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MANUEL ELAURIA et al VS. SUPERSHUTTLE INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 15, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANTS SUPERSHUTTLE INTERNATIONAL, INC., SUPERSHUTTLE SAN FRANCISCO, INC. AND TOM LEVOY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT OPPOSE THE DEMURRER ON THE ISSUE OF THE ARBITRATION AWARD BEING RES JUDICATA OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM BASED ON THE ISSUES ARBITRATED. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS, APART FROM S.S.S.F.'S CONDUCT, TO PROCEED AGAINST S.S.I. AND/OR LAVOY AND PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO DO SO. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCES OAKEN et al VS. LEO DONIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Xcomp
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 15, 2008 LINE 5. CROSS-COMPLAINANT LEO DONIAN'S MOTION For Leave To File First Amended CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART T0 ALLEGE A CLAIM OR CLAIMS BASED ON THE AUCTION AGREEMENT, HONIG V FINANCIAL CORP. OF AMERICA (1992) 6 CAL APP 4TH 960, 966 DOES ALLOW CLAIMS THAT ARISE AFTER THE COMPLAINT WAS FILED WHERE IT MAKES SENSE FOR THE PARTIES TO TRY THEIR RELATED DISPUTES AT ONE TIME. ANY CLAIM BASED ON THE LOAN IS UNTIMELY FOR IT WAS NOT MADE IN THE PROBATE PROCEEDINGS. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY PRICE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 15, 2008 LINE 13. DEFENDANT DAP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF USED DEFENDANT'S PRODUCTS, SOME CONTAINED ASBESTOS. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE DAP PLAINTIFF USED CONTAINED ASBESTOS OR THAT THE CONTACT PLAINTIFF HAD WITH DEFENDANT'S PRODUCT EXPOSED HIM TO ASBESTOS. THE RECORD IS SILENT AS TO WHETHER IT IS NECESSARY TO SAND DAP FOR ASBESTOS FIBERS TO BE RELEASED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY PRICE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

THE DECLARATIONS OF PLAINTIFF AND CRESTELO CREATE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON ISSUE OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES BASED ON THE TESTIMONY OF AMBLER AND HOROWITZ. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MARK WILLIAMS VS. VIA LEE LOW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike; Courtesy Copy Of Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 15, 2008 LINE 10. DEFENDANTS PAUL WARTELLE, FRANCIS PINNOCK AND WARTELLE, WEAVER & SCHREIBER'S Special Motion To Strike IS GRANTED. PRONG ONE IS MET BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT IS CENTERED ON DEFENDANT'S PRIOR CLAIM AGAINST PLAINTIFF. PRONG TWO IS MET. IN DORAN V MAGAN (1999) THE COURT HELD THAT THE DENIAL OF A C.C.P. 664.6 MOTION IS SIMPLY THE DENIAL OF THE MOTION AND DOES NOT DISPOSE OF THE ACTIONS. THE PARTIES RETAIN THE ABILITY TO CONTINUE TO LITIGATE THE ISSUES. THUS, A REASONABLE ATTORNEY WOULD BELIEVE THAT DEFENDANT'S ACTION COULD BE CONTINUE. THIS MEANS PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE HIS CLAIM OF MALICIOUS PROSECUTION. IT ALSO WAS NOT AN ABUSE OF PROCESS TO CONTINUE THE ACTION. THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE, CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(B), IS A DEFENSE TO BOTH THE ABUSE OF PROCESS AND INVASION OF PRIVACY CLAIMS. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: ASPEN SOUTH HILLS APARTMENTS A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. TACONIA MAXEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment Pursuant To Stip And Motion To Vacate Judgment Pursuant To Stip; (Alternatively) Motion For Relief From Forfeiture;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 15, 2008 LINE 12. DEFENDANT TACONIA MAXEY'S Motion To Vacate Judgment Pursuant To STIPULATION And Motion To Vacate Judgment Pursuant To STIPULATION; (Alternatively) Motion For Relief From Forfeiture IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED REPEATEDLY TO MAKE THE PAYMENTS REQUIRED BY THE PARTIES' STIPULATION. AT PRESENT, DEFENDANT WOULD BE REQUIRED TO PAY PLAINTIFF $3,856.25 TO MAKE PLAINTIFF WHOLE. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE IN THE RECORD THAT DEFENDANT IS CAPABLE OF DOING SO OR THAT DEFENDANT HAS THE FINANCIAL ABILITY TO MAKE FUTURE RENTAL PAYMENTS. =(302/PJM)
